key_insight: Acoustoelastic Imaging reconstructs internal stress fields directly from shear-wave propagation by treating stress recovery as a physics-informed inverse wave problem.
engineering_relevance: This enables non-destructive, sub-wavelength stress mapping in heterogeneous solids and tissues without needing an explicit constitutive model or prior material calibration.
method: Measure full-field shear waveforms in the specimen.
Use physics-informed full waveform inversion to recover wave-equation coefficients altered by pre-stress.
Infer stress magnitude and principal directions from the reconstructed wave dynamics.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical simulations and ultrasound shear wave elastography experiments, achieving accurate nonuniform stress reconstruction with spatial resolution below 0.28λ.
watch_item: Performance may depend on wavefield quality and inversion stability, so noisy measurements or strong modeling mismatch could limit robustness in real materials.
paper_title: Direct stress imaging from shear wave propagation
